---
title: "Module 5 — Linear Modeling"
format: html
editor: source
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

> *Your mode for this exercise.* Group A: **agentic** — describe each step to Pi and let it write the code. Group B: **manual** — write each step yourself. A chat LLM may answer conceptual questions but should not edit this notebook or run commands. Exercise 2 flips the modes.

```{r setup}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)

# Helper function for plotting
plot_timecourse <- function(data, y_col) {
  ggplot(data) +
    geom_point(aes(x = Timepoint, y = .data[[y_col]], col = Genotype)) +
    ylab(y_col)
}
```

```{r}
# Read gene data and rename/log-transform gene of interest
genes <- read_csv(here("data/modeling/01-sorghum_timecourse.csv")) |>
    mutate(log_gene = log(Sobic.001G062300.v3.1))
```

## Part 1 — Predict (no code)

```{r}
# visualize the data
plot_timecourse(genes, "log_gene")
```

## Part 2 — Build

```{r}
# step 1: fit linear model with Timepoint + Genotype
# Use lm() with formula log_gene ~ Timepoint + Genotype
```

```{r}
# step 2: examine model summary
# Use summary() on your fitted model
```

```{r}
# step 3: generate and plot simulations
# Use simulate() to create new response values
# Store in genes$simulated and plot with plot_timecourse()
```

## Part 3 — Reach (optional)

```{r}
# Analyze second gene
genes <- genes |>
    mutate(log_gene = log(Sobic.008G179900.v3.1))
plot_timecourse(genes, "log_gene")

# Fit model to second gene (modify formula if needed)
```
